formative_content_refs.title: Poetry Appreciation: Imagery and Tone Analysis
Speaking Activity: Role Play on Social Issues

practicals.title: Grammar Transformation Exercise
practicals.details: Practical exercise to transform direct speech into reported speech for commands, requests, statements, and questions.

practicals.title: Editing and Gap Filling Practice
practicals.details: Students will edit paragraphs and fill gaps to practice accurate usage of tenses, modals, and punctuation.

practicals.title: Visual Data Transcoding
practicals.details: Practical task to transcode information from a graph or chart into an analytical paragraph of 100-120 words.

practicals.title: Oral Communication Simulation
practicals.details: Simulate real-life oral communication scenarios such as interviews or public announcements to practice listening and speaking.

activities.title: Role Play and Dramatization
activities.details: Students will enact scenes from the play to understand character dynamics, humor, and dramatic irony.

activities.title: Poetry Recitation with Intonation
activities.details: Students will recite the poem focusing on rhythm, pause, and intonation to convey the poet's mood.

activities.title: Group Discussion on Contemporary Issues
activities.details: Students will discuss the themes of poverty, redemption, and social responsibility based on the text.

activities.title: Listening to Audio Texts
activities.details: Students will listen to authentic audio texts (weather reports, interviews) and retell the content.

activities.title: Speaking Presentation Skills
activities.details: Students will deliver short presentations on topics of mutual interest, focusing on fluency and clarity.

activities.title: Simulating Real Life Situations
activities.details: Students will simulate a scenario where they write and respond to official communications based on the story.

activities.title: Interpreting Visual Data
activities.details: Students will interpret charts and statistical data from the unseen passage to answer comprehension questions.

activities.title: Debate on Character Traits
activities.details: Students will debate the character traits of Sulekha (Bholi) and her transformation.

projects.title: Art-Integrated Role Play Project
projects.details: Create a skit or dramatization of the play 'The Proposal' incorporating art elements like costumes and props to assess listening and speaking skills.

projects.title: Multilingual Literature Appreciation Project
projects.details: Students will research and present on Indian languages and literature, comparing themes with the text 'The Lost Child'.

projects.title: Creative Writing Portfolio
projects.details: Compile a portfolio of creative writing pieces including stories, diary entries, and descriptive paragraphs based on visual cues.

projects.title: Listening and Speaking Skills Assessment Project
projects.details: Conduct a series of interactive speaking activities and listening tasks, assessed using the provided rubric for fluency, pronunciation, and vocabulary.

marks_distribution: Section A: Reading Skills - 20 Marks
Section B: Writing Skills and Grammar - 20 Marks
Section C: Language through Literature - 40 Marks
Internal Assessment (Listening & Speaking) - 5 Marks
question_paper_design: Reading Comprehension: Two unseen passages (Discursive 400-450 words, Case-based factual with visual input 200-250 words). Total 600-700 words. MCQs/Objective/Very Short Answer.
Grammar: Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-verb concord, Reported speech. Gap Filling/Editing/Transformation (10 out of 12 questions attempted).
Writing Skills: Descriptive Paragraph (100-120 words) OR Story/Diary Entry (100-120 words). One out of two.
Reference to Context: Extracts from Drama/Prose and Poetry. MCQs/Objective/Very Short Answer.
Short & Long Answer Questions: From BEHIVE/FIRST FLIGHT (Prose/Poems) and MOMENTS/FOOTPRINTS WITHOUT FEET (Collection). Interpretation, Analysis, Inference, Evaluation.
Internal Assessment: Listening and Speaking Skills (5 Marks).
competency_weightage: Reading Comprehension: Conceptual understanding, decoding, analyzing, inferring, interpreting, vocabulary (20 Marks)
Writing Skills & Grammar: Creative expression, reasoning, justifying, appropriate style/tone/format, fluency, accuracy (20 Marks)
Language through Literature: Recalling, reasoning, appreciating, applying literary conventions, identifying themes/messages (40 Marks)
internal_assessment: Integrated component of all four language skills
Emphasis on speaking and listening during assessment
project_assessment: Assessment parameters: Interactive competence (Initiation & turn taking, relevance), Fluency (cohesion, coherence, speed), Pronunciation, Language (grammar and vocabulary)
Rubric levels: Interaction quality, Fluency/Coherence, Pronunciation accuracy, Vocabulary/Grammar flexibility

practicals.title: Grammar Transformation Exercise
practicals.details: Practical exercise to transform direct speech into reported speech for commands, requests, statements, and questions.

practicals.title: Editing and Gap Filling Practice
practicals.details: Students will edit paragraphs and fill gaps to practice accurate usage of tenses, modals, and punctuation.

practicals.title: Visual Data Transcoding
practicals.details: Practical task to transcode information from a graph or chart into an analytical paragraph of 100-120 words.

practicals.title: Oral Communication Simulation
practicals.details: Simulate real-life oral communication scenarios such as interviews or public announcements to practice listening and speaking.

activities.title: Role Play and Dramatization
activities.details: Students will enact scenes from the play to understand character dynamics, humor, and dramatic irony.

activities.title: Poetry Recitation with Intonation
activities.details: Students will recite the poem focusing on rhythm, pause, and intonation to convey the poet's mood.

activities.title: Group Discussion on Contemporary Issues
activities.details: Students will discuss the themes of poverty, redemption, and social responsibility based on the text.

activities.title: Listening to Audio Texts
activities.details: Students will listen to authentic audio texts (weather reports, interviews) and retell the content.

activities.title: Speaking Presentation Skills
activities.details: Students will deliver short presentations on topics of mutual interest, focusing on fluency and clarity.

activities.title: Simulating Real Life Situations
activities.details: Students will simulate a scenario where they write and respond to official communications based on the story.

activities.title: Interpreting Visual Data
activities.details: Students will interpret charts and statistical data from the unseen passage to answer comprehension questions.

activities.title: Debate on Character Traits
activities.details: Students will debate the character traits of Sulekha (Bholi) and her transformation.

projects.title: Art-Integrated Role Play Project
projects.details: Create a skit or dramatization of the play 'The Proposal' incorporating art elements like costumes and props to assess listening and speaking skills.

projects.title: Multilingual Literature Appreciation Project
projects.details: Students will research and present on Indian languages and literature, comparing themes with the text 'The Lost Child'.

projects.title: Creative Writing Portfolio
projects.details: Compile a portfolio of creative writing pieces including stories, diary entries, and descriptive paragraphs based on visual cues.

projects.title: Listening and Speaking Skills Assessment Project
projects.details: Conduct a series of interactive speaking activities and listening tasks, assessed using the provided rubric for fluency, pronunciation, and vocabulary.

marks_distribution: Section A: Reading Skills - 20 Marks
Section B: Writing Skills and Grammar - 20 Marks
Section C: Language through Literature - 40 Marks
Internal Assessment (Listening & Speaking) - 5 Marks
question_paper_design: Reading Comprehension: Two unseen passages (Discursive 400-450 words, Case-based factual with visual input 200-250 words). Total 600-700 words. MCQs/Objective/Very Short Answer.
Grammar: Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-verb concord, Reported speech. Gap Filling/Editing/Transformation (10 out of 12 questions attempted).
Writing Skills: Descriptive Paragraph (100-120 words) OR Story/Diary Entry (100-120 words). One out of two.
Reference to Context: Extracts from Drama/Prose and Poetry. MCQs/Objective/Very Short Answer.
Short & Long Answer Questions: From BEHIVE/FIRST FLIGHT (Prose/Poems) and MOMENTS/FOOTPRINTS WITHOUT FEET (Collection). Interpretation, Analysis, Inference, Evaluation.
Internal Assessment: Listening and Speaking Skills (5 Marks).
competency_weightage: Reading Comprehension: Conceptual understanding, decoding, analyzing, inferring, interpreting, vocabulary (20 Marks)
Writing Skills & Grammar: Creative expression, reasoning, justifying, appropriate style/tone/format, fluency, accuracy (20 Marks)
Language through Literature: Recalling, reasoning, appreciating, applying literary conventions, identifying themes/messages (40 Marks)
internal_assessment: Integrated component of all four language skills
Emphasis on speaking and listening during assessment
project_assessment: Assessment parameters: Interactive competence (Initiation & turn taking, relevance), Fluency (cohesion, coherence, speed), Pronunciation, Language (grammar and vocabulary)
Rubric levels: Interaction quality, Fluency/Coherence, Pronunciation accuracy, Vocabulary/Grammar flexibility

Speaking Activity: Role Play on Social Issues

practicals.title: Grammar Transformation Exercise
practicals.details: Practical exercise to transform direct speech into reported speech for commands, requests, statements, and questions.

practicals.title: Editing and Gap Filling Practice
practicals.details: Students will edit paragraphs and fill gaps to practice accurate usage of tenses, modals, and punctuation.

practicals.title: Visual Data Transcoding
practicals.details: Practical task to transcode information from a graph or chart into an analytical paragraph of 100-120 words.

practicals.title: Oral Communication Simulation
practicals.details: Simulate real-life oral communication scenarios such as interviews or public announcements to practice listening and speaking.

activities.title: Role Play and Dramatization
activities.details: Students will enact scenes from the play to understand character dynamics, humor, and dramatic irony.

activities.title: Poetry Recitation with Intonation
activities.details: Students will recite the poem focusing on rhythm, pause, and intonation to convey the poet's mood.

activities.title: Group Discussion on Contemporary Issues
activities.details: Students will discuss the themes of poverty, redemption, and social responsibility based on the text.

activities.title: Listening to Audio Texts
activities.details: Students will listen to authentic audio texts (weather reports, interviews) and retell the content.

activities.title: Speaking Presentation Skills
activities.details: Students will deliver short presentations on topics of mutual interest, focusing on fluency and clarity.

activities.title: Simulating Real Life Situations
activities.details: Students will simulate a scenario where they write and respond to official communications based on the story.

activities.title: Interpreting Visual Data
activities.details: Students will interpret charts and statistical data from the unseen passage to answer comprehension questions.

activities.title: Debate on Character Traits
activities.details: Students will debate the character traits of Sulekha (Bholi) and her transformation.

projects.title: Art-Integrated Role Play Project
projects.details: Create a skit or dramatization of the play 'The Proposal' incorporating art elements like costumes and props to assess listening and speaking skills.

projects.title: Multilingual Literature Appreciation Project
projects.details: Students will research and present on Indian languages and literature, comparing themes with the text 'The Lost Child'.

projects.title: Creative Writing Portfolio
projects.details: Compile a portfolio of creative writing pieces including stories, diary entries, and descriptive paragraphs based on visual cues.

projects.title: Listening and Speaking Skills Assessment Project
projects.details: Conduct a series of interactive speaking activities and listening tasks, assessed using the provided rubric for fluency, pronunciation, and vocabulary.

marks_distribution: Section A: Reading Skills - 20 Marks

competency_groups.title: Reading Comprehension & Analysis
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to decoding, interpreting, analyzing, inferring, and evaluating unseen passages including discursive and case-based factual texts with visual inputs.
competencies: Decoding text meaning from discursive passages (400-450 words)
Analyzing case-based factual passages with statistical data or charts
Inferring implicit meanings and evaluating arguments in unseen texts
Identifying main points, significant details, and vocabulary in context

competency_groups.title: Grammar Application & Writing Skills
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving the accurate application of grammatical structures (tenses, modals, reported speech) and creative expression in writing formats.
competencies: Applying sequence of tenses, modals, and non-finites accurately
Transforming sentences and correcting errors in reported speech
Writing descriptive paragraphs based on visual or verbal cues
Constructing stories, diary entries, formal letters, and analytical paragraphs
Demonstrating appropriate style, tone, and fluency in written communication

competency_groups.title: Literary Appreciation & Interpretation
competency_groups.description: Competencies focused on recalling, reasoning, appreciating literary conventions, identifying themes, and extrapolating meaning from prose, poetry, and drama.
competencies: Interpreting extracts from Prose, Poetry, and Drama contexts
Identifying central themes, sub-themes, and the writer's message
Analyzing literary devices, imagery, tone, and character sketches
Extrapolating beyond the text to demonstrate creativity and imagination
Justifying interpretations through textual evidence

competency_groups.title: Listening & Speaking Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies for interactive communication, fluency, pronunciation, and language usage in oral settings.
competencies: Initiating conversations and taking turns appropriately
Demonstrating fluency with cohesion, coherence, and speed
Pronouncing words clearly with correct intonation
Using a wide range of appropriate vocabulary and grammatical structures
Participating in role plays, skits, and dramatizations

competency_groups.title: Workbook Language Proficiency
competency_groups.description: Competencies derived from workbook units focusing on expression, accuracy, and fluency in specific language exercises.
competencies: Demonstrating accuracy and fluency in structured language exercises
Applying style and tone appropriate to the context
Integrating grammatical structures with high professional grasp
